articles.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
articles.summary: Elon Musk may file for SpaceX's IPO, with the earliest expected date in June based on SEC review timelines. The article speculates on potential filing dates and IPO announcement timing.

articles.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, a daily online talk show featuring interviews with AI executives and tech leaders. The show streams on X and YouTube, competing with major financial networks.

articles.title: It’s not easy to get depression-detecting AI through the FDA
articles.summary: Kintsugi, a seven-year-old startup developing AI to detect depression and anxiety from speech, is shutting down after failing FDA clearance. The company will release its technology as open-source, potentially for uses beyond healthcare.

articles.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
articles.summary: Microsoft released three new foundational AI models capable of transcribing voice to text, generating audio, and creating images, following the group's formation six months ago.

articles.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
articles.summary: Google's Vids app now allows users to customize and direct avatars through text prompts for video creation, enhancing the video-making experience with AI-powered controls.

articles.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
articles.summary: Anthropic accidentally issued thousands of takedown notices targeting GitHub repositories containing leaked source code. The company retracted most notices after executives acknowledged the mistake.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a simpler alternative for the growing AI application market.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscriptions or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of applicants and helped recruit engineers in a competitive market.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Quantum computers require significantly fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic curve encryption. Recent research shows utility-scale quantum computers could crack 256-bit ECC in 10 days with 100 times less overhead.

articles.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
articles.summary: Google moved its Q Day deadline to 2029, when quantum computers could break current encryption. The company urges adoption of post-quantum cryptography to protect sensitive data.

articles.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
articles.summary: TeamPCP, a hacking group, spreads self-propagating malware targeting unsecured cloud platforms for data theft, ransomware, and cryptocurrency mining. The group includes a data wiper targeting Iranian machines.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from geopolitical conflict threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemicals become scarce. Americans may soon face increased plastic costs and availability issues.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions are now threatening plastic supply chains, as plastics depend on petrochemicals. This could impact global economy beyond energy costs.

articles.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
articles.summary: Gig workers worldwide, including medical students, record themselves performing daily tasks to train humanoid robots. Companies like Micro1 collect this video data and sell it to robotics firms developing humanoids.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device, enabling advanced intelligence capabilities locally without cloud dependency.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer interaction technology, advancing how users engage with digital systems through innovative interface design and improved usability.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Article content not provided. Unable to generate summary without the article text.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential applications. The conversation explores upcoming developments and challenges in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Major brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and boost creative capabilities in content creation and marketing initiatives.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available. The initiative aims to help farmers leverage advanced technology for sustainable and efficient farming practices.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI video generation powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling users to create, edit, and share high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with Brazil's government to create a satellite imagery map for forest protection. The initiative uses advanced imaging technology to monitor and preserve the country's forests.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in March 2026, showcasing new developments and capabilities in artificial intelligence technology and applications.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models yet, specifically designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model delivers improved precision and lower latency for more natural, fluid, and precise voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI-driven manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while the podcast discusses recent AI developments including Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that boosts photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate enhanced details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and Cursor's trigger-based coding automations.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is an enterprise platform addressing shadow AI by governing autonomous agents deployed outside official channels. It tackles the BYOAI trend where employees bypass procurement, exposing proprietary data to unregulated environments.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include enforcing strict access governance, encryption, and role-based permissions to protect sensitive AI models.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI development through 2030, focusing on high-performance chips, software, algorithms, and infrastructure like 5G+ and 6G networks to support AI deployment.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems. This addresses debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems designed for complex problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in localization, navigation, and path planning in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by successfully balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a longstanding challenge in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering and parabolic extrapolation enable LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators in scientific research, producing testable multiverse models.

articles.title: IBM Releases Granite 4.0 3B Vision: A New Vision Language Model for Enterprise Grade Document Data Extraction
articles.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a specialized vision-language model for enterprise document data extraction. The modular design prioritizes structured data accuracy over general image captioning.

articles.title: How to Build Production Ready AgentScope Workflows with ReAct Agents, Custom Tools, Multi-Agent Debate, Structured Output and Concurrent Pipelines
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building production-ready AgentScope workflows using ReAct agents, custom tools, multi-agent debates, structured outputs with Pydantic, and concurrent pipelines for parallel problem-solving.

articles.title: Z.ai Launches GLM-5V-Turbo: A Native Multimodal Vision Coding Model Optimized for OpenClaw and High-Capacity Agentic Engineering Workflows Everywhere
articles.summary: Zhipu AI launched GLM-5V-Turbo, a vision-language model combining visual perception with code execution through native multimodal fusion, designed for agentic engineering workflows.

articles.title: Android's emergency alerts just got a major map upgrade - but change this setting first
articles.summary: Android's emergency alerts now include an upgraded map view showing your location relative to affected areas, crucial for the upcoming US storm season.

articles.title: How I pay less for gas: 5 apps I use to find the cheapest stations nearby
articles.summary: Five apps help drivers find the cheapest gas stations nearby, providing real-time price comparisons to save money as fuel costs continue rising.

articles.title: Google's Gemma 4 model goes fully open-source and unlocks powerful local AI - even on phones
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4 as fully open-source under Apache 2.0, enabling offline multimodal AI deployment on servers, phones, and Raspberry Pi devices with complete local control.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from behind-the-scenes tools to more visible applications. Key trends for 2026 include advancements in ML deployment and integration across industries.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where human oversight pauses agent execution pipelines. This creates a human-in-the-loop approval mechanism for autonomous agents.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference phases: prefill attention mechanism, decode phase, and KV cache optimization for efficient token generation during prediction.

articles.title: Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem (Part 2: From Projections to Predictions)
articles.summary: Linear regression can be understood as a vector projection problem. This perspective provides geometric insight into how least squares fitting works for making predictions.

articles.title: How to Handle Classical Data in Quantum Models
articles.summary: The article explores workflows and encoding techniques for integrating classical data into quantum machine learning models, addressing a key challenge in hybrid quantum-classical computing.

articles.title: Quantum Simulations with Python
articles.summary: Learn how to run quantum experiments using Qiskit-Aer, a Python library for quantum simulations. This guide enables developers to conduct quantum computing experiments without requiring actual quantum hardware.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution on various devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time contextual processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a full 3D real-time strategy game.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that act as intelligent grid assets, enabling efficient power management and faster grid integration while improving system reliability.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables evaluation of multi-turn AI agents using realistic user simulations. The framework assesses agent interactions across helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage for production readiness.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using Amazon SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanding context windows, reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables secure AI agent web browsing by using AWS Network Firewall to control domain access and filter unauthorized websites, preventing data exfiltration and security risks.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 launches as multimodal and multilingual models scaling from data centers to edge devices, addressing deployment needs across the full spectrum.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI models enable ultra-low microsecond latency inference for algorithmic trading, allowing firms to compete in high-speed electronic markets. Advanced neural networks replace traditional specialized hardware like FPGAs and ASICs.

articles.title: CUDA Tile Programming Now Available for BASIC!
articles.summary: CUDA Tile Programming is now available for BASIC as an April Fools' joke that actually works, showcasing CUDA's flexibility. CUDA 13.1 introduces tile-based GPU programming for more accessible fine-grained parallelism.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences influence consumer attitudes toward ads across various social media platforms, exploring people's relationships with social media advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and making solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative XR technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify feature interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more understandable for building safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders to map objects to images, incorporating noise models to quantify measurement quality and distinguish between objects effectively.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, addressing scalability challenges and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
